Container.Name: yt-dlp-web
Container.Overview: Web interface for yt-dlp with organized file naming and progress tracking. Downloads videos with format: video-title-yyyy-mm-dd/video-title-yyyy-mm-dd.mp4
Container.Description:
A modern web interface for yt-dlp that organizes downloads with consistent naming.
🎯 Key Features: • Clean, responsive web UI for single/batch downloads • Organized folder structure: video-title-yyyy-mm-dd/video-title-yyyy-mm-dd.mp4 • Real-time progress tracking with job management • Support for 1000+ sites (YouTube, Vimeo, TikTok, Twitter, etc.) • Automatic MP4 format selection with quality preferences • Background processing with status monitoring • Docker health checks and logging • RESTful API for automation
🚀 Usage: 1. Access web interface at http://[unraid-ip]:8000 2. Paste video URL(s) and click download 3. Monitor progress in real-time 4. Files saved to organized folders in downloads directory
📁 File Structure: /downloads/Video-Title-2024-01-15/Video-Title-2024-01-15.mp4
🔧 Built with Flask, yt-dlp, and modern web technologies.

Config.Name: Downloads Directory
Config.Description: Directory where downloaded videos will be stored

Config.Name: Max Concurrent Downloads
Config.Description: Maximum number of simultaneous downloads (1-5 recommended)

Config.Name: User ID
Config.Description: User ID for file permissions (99=nobody, standard for Unraid)

Config.Name: Group ID
Config.Description: Group ID for file permissions (100=users, standard for Unraid)
